title: 10 best herbal tinctures for sunburn

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with sunburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically, aloe vera gel can reduce redness and swelling, while also calming the skin's itching and burning sensations. The herbal extract's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, promoting faster healing and reducing the appearance of sunburned skin. As a result, aloe tinctures provide quick relief from sunburn discomfort, allowing skin to recover and regenerate more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's gel contains enzymes like bradakininase that promote collagen synthesis and accelerate wound closure, helping to repair damaged skin cells and tissue caused by sunburn.

Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidants, including vitamins C and E, neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural healing processes.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the skin by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Aloe-barbadensis polysaccharides: Aloe-barbadensis polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.

Catechin: Catechin is a phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, thereby promoting skin healing and reducing the risk of long-term damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in aloe-emodin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sunburned skin.
Gel (from Leaves): This is the most widely used part of the Aloe vera plant, providing a soothing, moisturizing effect to sunburned skin.
Aloe Polysaccharides (from Leaves): These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties and can help accelerate the healing process of sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of fresh aloe vera gel and 500ml of 80% ethanol in separate containers.
Chop the aloe vera gel into small pieces and mix it with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for maceration.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the herbal aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness, swelling, and pain. The tincture's rich antioxidants also help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the risk of peeling and blistering. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids that have been shown to protect against UV-induced damage, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating sunburn discomfort and promoting healthy, radiant skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot Marigold tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, making it a natural remedy for soothing sun-kissed skin.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot Marigold's tincture has been shown to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis and inhibiting bacterial growth, which helps prevent infection and promotes healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with sunburn by reducing inflammation, which can alleviate redness and discomfort caused by sun exposure.
Carotenoids: Specifically, carotenoids like lycopene, found in Calendula, act as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to sunburn.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Calendula, such as calendulic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the skin from further damage after a sunburn.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are most commonly used to make tinctures for sunburn due to their high concentration of flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used as they contain similar compounds to the flowers, providing relief from sunburn and promoting skin healing.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures as they contain a mixture of flavonoids and triterpenoids that aid in skin protection and healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom and dry them immediately to preserve their potency.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container to separate the liquid from solids.
Discard the solids and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with sunburn because of its unique ability to accelerate skin healing and reduce inflammation. The allantoin present in comfrey tincture promotes cell growth, repair, and regeneration, which can help soothe and calm sunburned skin. Additionally, comfrey's anti-inflammatory properties can ease redness, itching, and discomfort associated with sunburn. By applying comfrey tincture topically, individuals can experience faster healing times, reduced risk of infection, and a more comfortable recovery from sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: Comfrey's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting healing.
Wound-healing: Comfrey's allantoin content stimulates collagen synthesis, promotes tissue repair, and enhances wound closure, helping to accelerate the recovery process of sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps with sunburn by promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation through its protective and regenerative properties.
Saponins: Assist in soothing sunburned skin by reducing inflammation and promoting the repair of damaged skin tissues.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and caffeic acid): Help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation and also exhibit antioxidant properties to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain allantoin, which helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing sunburn pain and inflammation.
Stems: They contain mucilage, a protective gel-like substance that can help to soothe and protect sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh symphytum officinale root and leaf material from a clean environment.
Clean the symphytum officinale material by gently washing it with cool water to remove impurities.
Chop the cleaned symphytum officinale material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Steep 1 part of chopped symphytum officinale in 2 parts of 80-proof vodka for 4 weeks to create tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid from solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with sunburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The plantain leaf has been used for centuries to treat skin irritations, including burns and sun damage. When applied topically or taken orally as a tincture, plantain extracts can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, plantain's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of long-term skin damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming irritated skin.

Antioxidant: Plantain tincture's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and contribute to sunburn, helping to protect the skin from further harm and promote healing.

Wound-healing: Plantain tincture's wound-healing properties aid in the repair and regeneration of damaged skin tissue, accelerating the process of repairing sunburned skin and promoting healthy, vibrant skin.
remedy_constituents: Apolipids: Apolipids in Plantago major tinctures help with sunburn by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Plantago major tinctures help with sunburn by protecting the skin from oxidative damage and reducing inflammation caused by UV radiation, thereby promoting skin health and reducing sunburn severity.

Asterane diterpenes: Asterane diterpenes in Plantago major tinctures help with sunburn by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help reduce sunburn symptoms and promote skin recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Seeds: Plantago major seeds contain mucilages, which can help to protect and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Roots: The roots of Plantago major contain antioxidants and other compounds that can help to neutralize free radicals, reduce inflammation, and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantago major leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated using scissors or a knife.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7 to 10 days at 30 degrees celsius.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a herb cutter.
Steep 1 part of the chopped plant material in 2 parts of 70 percent ethanol for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the tincture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with sunburn because it possesses anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. When applied topically, the active compounds in St John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with sunburn. Additionally, its natural moisturizing agents help to hydrate and nourish the skin, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. As a result, St John's Wort tinctures can provide rapid relief from sunburn symptoms, allowing you to recover quickly and enjoy the great outdoors again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort contains flavonoids and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort tincture help neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and damage to skin cells caused by sunburn.

Vasodilatory effects: Hypericin in St. John's Wort can help dilate blood vessels, which may reduce warmth, itching, and discomfort associated with sunburn by improving circulation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and damage to skin cells caused by sunburn.

Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): As strong antioxidants, flavonoids in Hypericum perforatum can neutralize free radicals produced by UV radiation, thereby protecting skin cells from oxidative damage and promoting skin healing.

Phenolic Acids (e.g., Chlorogenic Acid, Caffeic Acid): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain, itching, and the risk of secondary infections associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe sunburned skin.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in soothing sunburned skin and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh plant tops and leaves early in the morning when they are dry.
Chop 1 part of plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or pruning shears.
Steep 1 part of chopped plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to infuse in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with sunburn because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tincture's natural cooling properties help to reduce heat, redness, and itching associated with sunburns, providing quick relief from discomfort. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant-rich profile helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV damage, promoting skin rejuvenation and reducing the risk of long-term sun damage. As a result, using herbal stinging nettle tinctures can provide effective natural relief from sunburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tincture contains compounds like neryl acetate and caffeic acid that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: The tannins present in stinging nettle tincture act as antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals caused by UV radiation and preventing further damage to the skin cells, reducing the risk of long-term sun damage.

Vasoconstrictive properties: Stinging nettle tincture contains compounds that can constrict blood vessels, which helps reduce excessive blood flow and subsequent swelling associated with sunburn, allowing the skin to heal faster.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an antioxidant to help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by sunburn, promoting skin repair and soothing discomfort.

Allicin: Although not the primary constituent of Urtica dioica, allicin (found in small amounts) is a sulfur compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate sunburn symptoms and accelerate skin recovery.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help mitigate sunburn damage by neutralizing free radicals, reducing inflammation, and supporting the skin's natural healing process.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for sunburn relief due to their high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and other compounds that help soothe and calm the skin.
Roots: Roots are also used to make tinctures for sunburn, as they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce pain and promote healing.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used in combination with leaves to make a tincture, as they contain some of the same beneficial compounds and can add to the overall effectiveness of the remedy.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried urtica dioica leaves and flowers and 2 parts high-proof vodka for a 1:2 ratio.
Combine the dried urtica dioica mixture with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the herbal urtica dioica tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tannins present in dandelion help to reduce redness and swelling, while the antioxidants work to repair damaged skin cells. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate blood flow can help to promote the removal of toxins from the skin, reducing further inflammation and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from sunburned skin without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation and reduce oxidative stress.
Wound-healing properties: Dandelion's antimicrobial and antifungal compounds can promote wound healing by preventing infection and promoting tissue repair, which is beneficial for sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing and soothing skin irritation.

Quercetin: A flavonoid phenolic compound that exhibits potent antioxidant properties, neutralizing free radicals that cause oxidative stress and damage to skin cells after sun exposure, thereby protecting the skin from further harm.

Sitosterol: A phytosterol that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by sunburn, while also promoting skin regeneration and cell protection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to soothe sunburned skin.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that help to calm and reduce inflammation in sunburned skin.
Roots: They are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help to relieve sunburn pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them thoroughly in a dark place.
Combine 1 part of dried taraxacum officinale with 2 parts of 95% vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the taraxacum officinale tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it out of direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sunflower tinctures helps with sunburn because of its unique composition, rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that work together to soothe and calm the skin. The tannins present in sunflowers help reduce inflammation, while the vitamin E content promotes skin regeneration and repair. Additionally, sunflower's anti-inflammatory properties ease discomfort and itching associated with sunburn, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving sun-kissed woes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sunflower tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linoleic acid and oleic acid, which help reduce inflammation and swelling caused by sunburn, alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich properties of sunflower tincture neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, protecting skin cells from damage and reducing the risk of long-term skin problems like premature aging and skin cancer.

Soothing and calming effects: Sunflower tincture contains flavonoids and other compounds that have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, helping to reduce redness, itching, and irritation caused by sunburn, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help protect the skin from UV damage, reduce inflammation, and promote collagen synthesis, all of which aid in soothing and healing sunburned skin.

Phenolic acids (including Ferulic acid): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with sunburn.

Tannins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, promoting a faster recovery and minimizing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a high concentration of oil that can help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Leaves: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in reducing redness and promoting skin healing.
Flowers: They have a cooling effect and can provide relief from sunburn due to their anti-inflammatory properties and soothing action.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the sunflower flowers when in full bloom and dry them completely in a warm place for 7 days.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife and a clean cutting board.
Combine the chopped flowers with 80 proof vodka or 95% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar in a cool dark place for 4 weeks or 28 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with sunburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in chamomile work to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburned skin. By applying a topical tincture or consuming it internally, the body absorbs the beneficial compounds, which calm the skin and promote healing. This natural remedy can provide relief from itching, stinging, and burning sensations, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced risk of further irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, allowing for faster recovery and relief from pain.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in chamomile neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and contribute to premature aging, and also protect against further damage.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's calming effects help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common side effects of sunburn, while its soothing properties alleviate itching, burning sensations, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with sunburn by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, which can aid in the recovery process.
Bisabolol: As a sesquiterpene alcohol, bisabolol has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe sunburned skin, reduce redness, and promote skin regeneration.
α-Bisabolol acetate: This sesquiterpene ester also exhibits anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help alleviate discomfort and promote the healing of sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to alleviate sunburn pain and irritation.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to prevent infection and reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to provide a deep sense of calm and relaxation, which can help to reduce stress and promote the body's natural healing processes in response to sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are at their highest potency and quality.
Clean and dry the flowers thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris in 24 to 48 hours.
Steep 1 part of the dried flowers in 2 parts of high-proof spirit like vodka for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before using it to allow the flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with sunburn because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that quickly soothe and calm the skin. The herb's unique properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn, allowing for faster healing and relief from pain. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antioxidant-rich extracts help to protect the skin from further damage caused by UV radiation, promoting healthy skin regeneration and a reduced risk of long-term damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas Coneflower tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds in Kansas Coneflower tincture help protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate aging and worsen sunburn symptoms.

Wound-healing stimulation: The saponins present in Kansas Coneflower tincture have been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve wound healing, helping to reduce the appearance of peeling and promote a smoother skin recovery.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, Cichoric acid and Alkaloids of the isoquinoline and benzylisoquinoline classes): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn, promoting faster healing and skin repair.

Iridoid Glycosides (specifically, Echinacoside): Echinacoside has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from further damage and promote the healing process after sunburn.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin and Kaempferol): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by sunburn, promoting faster recovery and skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in soothing and reducing sunburn pain.
Leaves: Contain antioxidants that help protect the skin from further damage and promote healing.
Buds: May possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest roots of echinacea angustifolia in late fall or early spring when plants are dormant for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested roots to a moisture content of 10% in a warm well-ventilated area for 2-4 weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and store the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with sunburn because it contains flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the affected skin, these compounds help to soothe and calm the burn, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, licorice root extract has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, further alleviating discomfort and promoting faster recovery from sun damage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: Licorice root's antioxidant properties can neutralize free radicals generated by UV radiation, which can damage skin cells and contribute to sunburn.
Soother: The mucilages present in licorice root can help calm and soothe the skin, providing relief from discomfort, pain, and heat associated with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm skin irritation caused by sunburn.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and 700 milliliters of vodka at 40% abv.
Combine the dried root material with the vodka in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks.
Shake the mixture daily for its entire duration to facilitate extraction and mixing processes efficiently.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container after six weeks of steeping process completion.
Transfer the filtered tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort. The flavonoids present in chicory have been shown to scavenge free radicals, reducing oxidative stress caused by UV radiation. Additionally, the tannins in chicory have astringent and antiseptic properties that help to calm and protect the skin from further irritation. This natural remedy can provide fast relief from sunburned skin, promoting a faster recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal chicory tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant: Chicory's antioxidant compounds can neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, protecting the skin from further damage and promoting natural healing.
Skin soothing: The tincture's astringent and cooling properties can help calm irritated skin, reduce itching and pruritus, and promote a sense of comfort after sun exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chicory plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling.
Barks: The barks of the chicory plant have been traditionally used to treat skin irritations and burns, making them a popular choice for medicinal tinctures targeting sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and wash cichorium intybus leaves in cold water to remove dirt and impurities.
Chop 50g of fresh cichorium intybus leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine chopped leaves with 100ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 6 weeks of maceration time.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container discarding solids.
Store the clear cichorium intybus tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature forever.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with sunburn because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin. The tannins present in marshmallow root have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Additionally, the tincture's hydrating properties help to replenish moisture lost due to excessive heat, leaving skin feeling soft and supple. By applying a few drops of the herbal tincture topically, individuals can quickly alleviate discomfort and promote faster healing after a sunburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilage, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.

Soothing and protective properties: The mucilages in marshmallow tincture create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, providing relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations caused by sunburn.
Hydrating and moisturizing properties: Marshmallow tincture helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and peeling associated with sunburn, while also promoting the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps calm and moisturize sunburned skin.
Seeds: Contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce redness and swelling associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at low heat for 2 hours.
Chop the dried herb into small pieces and combine with vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid at room temperature for 14 days.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters and store in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ribwort tinctures helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's active compounds, such as hyperoside and quercetin, work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with sunburn. Additionally, ribwort tincture's hydrating effects help to replenish the skin's natural moisture barrier, promoting faster healing and relief from itching and stinging sensations. As a natural remedy, ribwort tincture offers a gentle and effective way to alleviate sunburn symptoms without harsh chemicals or artificial additives.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ribwort tincture contains compounds like salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with sunburn, making it a natural pain reliever.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of ribwort tincture help to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin cell repair.

Sobering and anti-allergic: Ribwort tincture has been traditionally used to soothe sunburned skin and reduce itching and redness due to its ability to calm down the nervous system and reduce histamine release.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids that aid in reducing inflammation, itching, and redness associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago lanceolata leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Rinse harvested leaves with distilled water and gently pat dry with paper towels.
Chop 500 grams of plant material into small pieces and combine with 1000 milliliters of vodka at a ratio of one part herb to three parts menstruum.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid for four weeks, shaking daily for 10 minutes to ensure thorough maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and label with date prepared and contents.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the skin. The herbal extract contains flavonoids and saponins that work together to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by UV exposure. Additionally, eyebright's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals formed during sun damage, promoting a quicker healing process and reducing the appearance of blisters and peeling. This natural remedy provides relief from the burning sensation, itching, and dryness associated with sunburn, allowing for a smoother recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which helps to alleviate redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with sunburn.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in eyebright tincture help neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, reducing oxidative stress and damage to skin cells.
Sotholing and soothing properties: Eyebright's mucilage content helps to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from burning sensation, itching, and discomfort often experienced with sunburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sunburned skin, reducing redness and swelling.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds contain antioxidants that protect against oxidative stress caused by UV radiation, promoting skin health and reducing the risk of damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring at least 20 grams of plant material.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces, removing any excess stem or debris, and place it in a clean glass jar.
Add 40% to 60% ethanol vodka or grain alcohol, pouring enough to cover the plant material by about an inch, and seal the jar tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark location for four to six weeks, shaking gently every few days, allowing the mixture to macerate and extract the active compounds.
After the maceration period, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the plant material, and label the bottle with the date of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated, ensuring freshness and potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold running water to remove dirt and debris, then gently shake off excess moisture.
Combine 100 grams of foeniculum vulgare plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring proper ratio of herb to solvent.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least four weeks, shaking it daily, to allow for thorough extraction and maceration process.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and transferring liquid tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh baptisia tinctoria leaves and flowers, carefully selecting only the top-quality material.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with 60% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day for optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 5%.
Chop the dried herb into small pieces and mix with 80% ethanol or vodka in a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer it to amber glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality oregano leaves from a reputable source in sufficient quantity for tincture production.
Chop the harvested oregano leaves into small pieces using scissors or a herb chopper for optimal extraction.
Combine one part of the chopped oregano leaves with five parts of pure grain vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to enhance solvent penetration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the product.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for sunburn are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate sunburn symptoms, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and discomfort. The best herbal tinctures for sunburn include Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, Symphytum officinale, Hamamelis virginiana, Avena sativa, Plantago major, and Echinacea purpurea. If left untreated, sunburn can lead to severe discomfort, increased risk of skin cancer, and long-term damage to skin cells, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to sunburn that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin irritations and inflammatory conditions. For example, hives, eczema, and rosacea can be alleviated by using tinctures made from anti-inflammatory herbs like calendula, chamomile, and plantain. These herbs help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, tinctures of soothing herbs like lavender and peppermint can provide relief from minor burns, cuts, and scrapes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with sunburn is Aloe Vera, Calendula, and Lavender. Aloe Vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Calendula's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help prevent infection and speed up the recovery process. Lavender's cooling and calming effects ease pain and discomfort, while also promoting relaxation. Combining these tinctures can help reduce redness, itching, and peeling, while promoting a faster and more comfortable recovery from sunburn.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Expert opinion on therapeutic patents, researchers conducted a patent search using databases such as WIPO, Espacenet, USPTO, and Derwent to identify potential photoprotective agents. The study found 180 patents, out of which 25 were evaluated for their application of natural products with photoprotective or photoaging applications. These natural products, often in the form of cosmetic compositions like lotions, gels, or creams, have been shown to be effective in preventing or reducing the signs of photoaging and sunburn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. It contains triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids that help calm and protect the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. The tincture promotes skin regeneration and wound healing, allowing the skin to recover faster from sun damage. Additionally, its antiseptic qualities help prevent infection and promote a healthy skin environment, making it an effective and natural remedy for sunburn relief. This natural remedy provides quick and lasting relief for sunburn sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: They help with sunburn by reducing inflammation and acting as antioxidants to protect the skin from further damage.

Sesquiterpenes: They help with sunburn by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which helps to soothe and calm sunburned skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which help soothe and protect sunburned skin.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their ability to calm and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for topical applications to relieve sunburn discomfort.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds are sometimes employed for their oil content, which is rich in fatty acids that can aid in skin regeneration and moisturizing sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part calendula flowers and 2 parts vegetable glycerin to create the tincture base.
Combine the calendula flowers and glycerin in a clean glass jar and seal it.
Place the jar in a cool dark location and steep for 2-3 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark location and let it mature for 4-6 weeks before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture, also known as St. John's Wort, contains hypericin and hyperforin, which help to soothe and calm sunburned skin. It reduces redness, swelling, and pain, making it an effective natural remedy for sunburn relief. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from further damage, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of long-term skin damage. This makes it an ideal natural remedy for sunburned skin.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a powerful antioxidant that helps to neutralize free radicals caused by UV radiation, thereby reducing oxidative stress and promoting skin healing.
Kaempferol: Kaempferol has anti-inflammatory and antiproliferative properties, which help to reduce skin inflammation and prevent cell damage caused by sunburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and tannins, which are believed to help soothe and calm sunburned skin.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin and other bioactive compounds, they are thought to help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in sunburned areas.
Buds: The buds contain similar compounds to the flowers and leaves, making them a valuable addition to sunburn-relief tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers of hypericum perforatum in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm dark place for at least two weeks to preserve the herb.
Measure out 250 grams of the dried flowers and combine with 500 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in the dark for four to six weeks shaking the jar every few days to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with sunburn because of its cooling and soothing properties. Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the skin and reduce redness associated with sunburn.

Aloin from Leaves: Aloin, a compound found in the leaves of Aloe vera, is sometimes used in tinctures for sunburn as it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with sunburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100% organic aloe vera leaves with no signs of mold or damage for making herbal tinctures.
Chop 2 pounds of aloe vera leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or kitchen shears.
Combine chopped aloe vera with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a large glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200g of mature aloe barbadensis leaves at dawn when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and weigh them to ensure the correct dosage of 1:5.
Combine the chopped leaves with 500ml of 70% vodka in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14-21 days in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh arnica montana flowers in mid-summer when they are in full bloom.
Dry the arnica montana flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2-3 weeks.
Grind 50 grams of dried arnica montana flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered arnica montana with 250 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks then strain and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh plant material in the early morning and clean it thoroughly with water.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to release its medicinal properties and increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of high-proof ethanol 95% in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the solvent to extract compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which help to calm and soothe sunburned skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried hamamelis virginiana roots and 2 parts of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves.
Combine the dried hamamelis virginiana roots and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Fill a clean glass jar with 80 proof vodka and pour it over the plant material.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks and shake it every day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of avena sativa roots and stems in the early morning or late evening to ensure high concentration of active compounds.
Chop the harvested avena sativa plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container to create a 50% solution.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the solvent to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major tinctures helps with sunburn because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 1-2 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with sunburn because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves on a sunny day in late summer.
Dry the fresh material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 100g of dried echinacea with 500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.
